text:
There was a small town with its own park. Every weekend, children would gather there. One day, a young girl named Lily came to the park every weekend. She didn't know many people, but she made good friends with an elderly widower named Mr. Jenkins.

One day, while playing, Lily noticed Mr. Jenkins was very quiet. When she approached him, he said, "Oh, dear Lily. How fortunate I am to have you spend time here each week."

"How are you?" she asked.

"I have lots to live for," replied Mr. Jenkins thoughtfully. "I enjoy my grandchildren."

"Lots?" Lily asked.

"Yes," Mr. Jenkins said with a warm smile. "My little ones. They're like little treasures."

One day, Lily played so energetically that she inadvertently scared one of the pigeons into flying away. Grief-stricken, she told Mr. Jenkins.

"I've lost something dear," she cried.
